metadata.name: Dyslexia Support Skill
metadata.description: Clinical and accommodation framework for dyslexia as a neurodevelopmental cognitive disorder — a lifelong, brain-based difference in phonological and orthographic processing architecture (left temporoparietal cortex, visual word form area, inferior frontal gyrus). Covers the full lifespan: K-12 special education (IDEA, Section 504, IDA standards), higher education (Section 504, ADA Title II/III), workplace (ADA Title I), and adult accommodation. Distinguishes remediation targets (trainable skills) from bypass targets (persistent deficits requiring permanent cognitive prosthetics). Grounded in Shaywitz phonological deficit theory, Dehaene VWFA research, Gabrieli neuroimaging evidence, DSM-5 Specific Learning Disorder (315.00), and ICD-11 Developmental Learning Disorder (6A03.0).
metadata.author: Justice

skills.name: standards-compliance
skills.description: Regulatory compliance framework treating dyslexia as a neurodevelopmental cognitive disorder across the full lifespan. Covers clinical frameworks (DSM-5 Specific Learning Disorder 315.00, ICD-11 Developmental Learning Disorder 6A03.0), IDA Knowledge and Practice Standards for Teachers of Reading 2018 (all 5 domains: foundation concepts, reading profiles, assessment, structured literacy teaching, ethics), K-12 special education law (IDEA specific learning disability identification, IEP requirements, FAPE, procedural safeguards), Section 504 (K-12 accommodations, eligibility differences from IDEA, major life activity determinations), higher education (Section 504 at postsecondary institutions, ADA Title II for public colleges, ADA Title III for private colleges, Office for Civil Rights complaint procedures), and workplace (ADA Title I reasonable accommodations, undue hardship analysis, disability disclosure, EEOC procedures). Includes neuroscience-informed Cognitive Disorder Accommodation checklist distinguishing between trainable skills and persistent deficits requiring permanent cognitive prosthetics.

skills.name: gap-analysis
skills.description: Systematic gap identification across the dyslexia cognitive support ecosystem, spanning education, clinical practice, workplace, and consumer technology. Identifies 10 critical gaps including retroactive vs. proactive accessibility, absence of personalized accommodation engines, passive vs. interactive assistive tools, fragmented tool ecosystems, disconnected progress monitoring, assessment-to-instruction pipeline breakdowns, practitioner implementation support gaps, missing dyslexia-specific UX design standards, workplace accommodation gaps (most employers lack dyslexia-specific AT policies), and clinical-educational framing gaps (educational systems treat dyslexia as remediable while neuroscience shows persistent cognitive architecture). Evaluates gaps against legal frameworks, cognitive neuroscience evidence, and adult/workplace outcomes. Includes pre-built measures: Dyslexia-Friendly Content Audit Checklist, Dyslexia UX Heuristics (12 heuristics extending Nielsen's 10), and Cognitive Prosthetics Maturity Model.

skills.name: remediation-strategies
skills.description: Evidence-based intervention and cognitive prosthetics framework for dyslexia grounded in structured literacy and Orton-Gillingham methodology. Explicitly distinguishes two target types: (1) **Remediation targets** — trainable skills that improve with instruction (phonological awareness, decoding accuracy, fluency, spelling patterns, morphological knowledge), addressed through structured literacy's six elements (phonology, sound-symbol association, syllable instruction, morphology, syntax, semantics) following OG principles (multisensory, explicit, diagnostic/prescriptive, sequential/cumulative, individualized), implemented via evidence-based programs (Wilson Reading System, Barton, Lindamood-Bell LiPS, Sonday, RAVE-O, Alphabetic Phonics); and (2) **Bypass targets** — persistent cognitive processing differences requiring permanent cognitive prosthetics (text-to-speech for reading load, speech-to-text for writing, spell/grammar checkers, audiobook access via Learning Ally and Bookshare, OCR for scanned materials, reading rulers, dyslexia-friendly fonts, real-time captioning, extended time as neurological accommodation not preference). Includes lifespan application from K-12 tutoring through adult workplace AT stacks.

skills.name: document-generation
skills.description: Professional document templates for the full lifespan of dyslexia accommodation needs. **K-12 educational**: IEPs (reading-focused with PLAAFP, SMART goals for phonological awareness/decoding/fluency/comprehension, services, accommodations including cognitive prosthetics, LRE), Section 504 Accommodation Plans, Progress Monitoring Reports, Prior Written Notice (PWN), RTI/Intervention Documentation. **Higher education**: Section 504/ADA disability services request letters, accommodation letters to faculty, testing accommodation requests, note-taking and recording permission documentation. **Workplace**: ADA Title I reasonable accommodation request letters (to HR), disability disclosure guidance, job description accommodation analysis, interactive process documentation, EEOC charge preparation. **Clinical**: Psychoeducational evaluation summaries with assessment instruments reference (CTOPP-2, WRMT-III, GORT-5, KTEA-3, WISC-V, Nelson-Denny for adults), DSM-5 diagnostic summary letters, cognitive prosthetics prescription/recommendation letters. **General**: Dyslexia screening checklists (early childhood, school-age, adult), parent/self-advocacy communication letters.

commands.name: dyslexia-audit
commands.description: Audit a program, tool, curriculum, workplace policy, or content for compliance with dyslexia cognitive accommodation requirements. Evaluates against IDA Knowledge and Practice Standards, structured literacy fidelity, and the applicable legal framework: IDEA/504 for K-12, Section 504/ADA for higher education, ADA Title I for workplace, ADA Title III for public accommodations. Generates comprehensive audit report with findings, composite score (A-F grade), and remediation roadmap covering structured literacy coverage, cognitive prosthetics availability, accommodation adequacy, and UDL principles. Creates audit files in audits/ subdirectory.

project_name.description: Name of the project, material, program, workplace policy, or tool being audited

context.description: Regulatory and population context for audit

commands.name: generate-iep
commands.description: Generate a comprehensive, legally-compliant accommodation document tailored to the individual's context. Supports four document types: (1) **IEP** — K-12 special education with PLAAFP, SMART goals for phonological awareness/decoding/fluency/comprehension/writing, structured literacy-aligned services, cognitive prosthetics accommodations, LRE documentation; (2) **504 Plan** — K-12 or higher education accommodations; (3) **Workplace Accommodation Request** — ADA Title I reasonable accommodation letter to employer/HR with documentation of functional impact, specific requested accommodations, and interactive process invitation; (4) **Higher Ed Disability Services Request** — university disability office documentation with academic accommodations, testing modifications, and assistive technology requests. Includes comorbidity considerations (ADHD, dyscalculia, anxiety, APD, executive function).

person_name.description: Individual's full name (student, employee, or self-advocate)

grade_or_role.description: Current grade level, program/major, or job title

cognitive_prosthetics.description: Cognitive prosthetics to include in accommodations

commands.name: remediation-plan
commands.description: Create a comprehensive support plan with TWO parallel tracks: (1) **Remediation track** — structured literacy instruction addressing trainable skills via the six elements (phonology, sound-symbol association, syllable instruction, morphology, syntax, semantics) and OG principles (multisensory, explicit, diagnostic/prescriptive, sequential/cumulative, individualized), implemented through evidence-based programs (Wilson, Barton, LiPS, Sonday, RAVE-O, Alphabetic Phonics); (2) **Cognitive prosthetics track** — permanent assistive technology stack addressing persistent processing differences (text-to-speech, speech-to-text, audiobook access via Learning Ally/Bookshare, OCR tools, spell/grammar checkers, dyslexia-friendly fonts, reading rulers). Includes lesson templates for remediation track, AT setup and training for prosthetics track, progress monitoring for both, and lifespan adaptations (K-12 through adult/workplace).

current_level.description: Current reading level and profile description

intervention_duration.description: Intervention duration (use 'ongoing' for adult cognitive prosthetics plans)

intensity.description: Intensity level (Tier 1/2/3 for K-12, session frequency for adult)

focus_areas.description: Focus areas. Use 'cognitive-prosthetics-only' for adults where remediation is not the primary goal

prosthetics_stack.description: Assistive technology stack: TTS tools, STT tools, audiobook services, OCR, etc.

anxiety_considerations.description: Include reading or disclosure anxiety management strategies

commands.name: content-accessibility-check
commands.description: Audit educational, workplace, or consumer content for dyslexia-friendly design across 10 dimensions: typography (font family, size, spacing, weight — dyslexic-friendly fonts like Lexend, Atkinson Hyperlegible, OpenDyslexic), readability (Flesch-Kincaid level, sentence length, cognitive load), color and contrast (WCAG compliance, off-white backgrounds, low-contrast pairs), layout (whitespace, line length 45-65 characters, left-alignment), cognitive load (chunking, scaffolding, working memory demands), navigation predictability, assistive technology compatibility (screen reader semantic HTML, text-to-speech parseability, proper heading hierarchy), multimedia accessibility (captions, transcripts, audio descriptions), UDL principles application, and cognitive accommodation integration. Applies to K-12 textbooks, higher-ed courseware, workplace training, consumer apps, and government/healthcare communications.

content_name.description: Name or description of the content being checked

templates.name: audit-report-template
templates.description: Standard template for dyslexia cognitive accommodation audit reports covering IDA standards, structured literacy compliance, WCAG 2.1 AA, ADA where applicable, and dyslexia-specific UX heuristics
